# Upstream LiveDemo — Auth Model

> Source of truth derived from `livedemo/livedemo-backend:latest` (image digest
> `sha256:6e63e427ac9f602a9057f6d20c2a0094b2e849c33b75c4d69b0eac85b05a43bb`).
> Extracted live at 2026-04-23 from `/home/app/src/` inside the running Railway
> container, cross-checked against a local copy of the same image.

---

## Token format

**Opaque 64-character hex string.** Generated by `crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex')`
in `src/helpers/authUtils.js` → `createTokenForUser()` → `randomValueHex(64)`.

Example captured at bootstrap:
```
2a163442...e1d0d2
```
(Full value lives in Infisical at `LIVEDEMO_API_TOKEN` and on `livedemo-mcp`
as the Railway env var of the same name. Redacted to first-8…last-6 for
this doc.)

Not a JWT — there is no header, payload, or signature; the token carries no
claims. The only way to interpret it is to look it up in the `authtokens`
Mongo collection.

## Acquisition

### Route
`POST /users/password-authenticate`

### Request
```json
{
  "email":    "mcp@deltakinetics.io",
  "password": "<plaintext>"
}
```

### Response 200
```json
{
  "id":               "69ea6d7d10a3c3c5d93195b3",
  "email":            "mcp@deltakinetics.io",
  "timezone":         "",
  "name":             "DK MCP Bot",
  "token":            "2a163442...e1d0d2",
  "workspaceMembers": [],
  "redirectPath":     "/onboarding"
}
```

> **Token field name: `token`.** (Not `authToken`, `accessToken`, `bearer`, or
> `access_token`.) Verified against bootstrap response captured in
> `discovery-log.md` §Phase-0, and against source at
> `src/handlers/postPasswordAuthenticate.js:64–72`.

### Alternative acquisition — signup side-effect
`POST /users` creates the user, creates a default workspace, creates an auth
token, and returns the same fields as `/users/password-authenticate`. See
`src/handlers/postUsers.js`. Signup is idempotent only in the sense that a
duplicate email returns an error (not 409 — see "Errors" below); the first
successful signup emits the token.

## Validation (how the backend verifies an incoming token)

From `src/helpers/livedemoHelpers.js:302–356` (`authReq`) and
`src/helpers/authHelpers.js` (`getUserByAccessToken`):

1. Middleware reads `req.get('Authorization')`.
2. Extracts the token with `authHeader.split(' ')[1]` — i.e. **expects
   `Authorization: Bearer <token>`**. A header that is just the raw token
   (no `Bearer ` prefix) still yields `undefined` at `[1]` and is rejected.
3. Looks up `AuthToken.findOne({ token, status: 'active' })`.
4. Looks up `User.findOne({ _id: authTokenDoc.userId })` populated with
   `workspaceMembers subscriptions workspaces`.
5. Attaches `{ authUser, authToken }` to the request pipeline.

Any failure → handler throws an error whose `resultResponse` is a 401 with
an empty body (header-only). See §"Edge cases observed" for the exact shape.

## Lifetime

**Long-lived; no expiry timestamp.** The `authtokens` schema
(`src/models/AuthToken.js`) has only `{ token, type, status, clientId,
authorizedInstances, scopes }` plus Mongoose `createdAt` / `updatedAt`
timestamps. There is **no `expiresAt`, no TTL index** on the collection
(no source defines one; index list to be confirmed once Mongo is
live-inspected — see UNVERIFIED block below).

A token is invalidated only when its `status` flips from `'active'` to
`'expired'`. The only code path that does this is `postLogout.js` — a
user-initiated logout. No scheduled job expires tokens.

**Practical implication:** the MCP service's `LIVEDEMO_API_TOKEN` is
effectively permanent until either (a) Matty logs the `mcp@deltakinetics.io`
user out of a browser session or (b) the user is deleted. No refresh logic
is required.

## Refresh strategy for the MCP server

**None required on a schedule.** Recommended handling:

1. On startup, read `LIVEDEMO_API_TOKEN` from env.
2. On any 401 from the upstream, re-authenticate with
   `POST /users/password-authenticate` using `LIVEDEMO_MCP_PASSWORD`, cache
   the new token in memory, and retry once.
3. Do **not** write the refreshed token back to Infisical/Railway from the
   running service — that's a deployment concern, not a runtime one.

`POST /users/refreshToken` exists (`postRefreshToken.js`) but requires an
already-valid token and is designed for session rotation — it is not
necessary for a service account.

## Edge cases observed

All captured live against the backend at `http://localhost:3005` inside
`railway ssh --service livedemo-backend` during the bootstrap flow
(2026-04-23).

### Missing Authorization header
- **Request:** `GET /workspaces` with no header.
- **Response:** status `401`, body empty string.
- **Source of shape:** `livedemoHelpers.js:308–319` — the `resultResponse`
  has only headers, `body` is `undefined`.

### Malformed `Authorization` header (no `Bearer ` prefix)
- **Request:** `GET /workspaces` with
  `Authorization: <raw-64-hex-token>`.
- **Response:** status `401`, body empty string.
- **Why:** `authHeader.split(' ')[1]` is `undefined`, handler throws
  "Authorization token not found".

### Valid `Bearer` header with an **unknown** token (e.g. stale `PRIVATE_AUTH_TOKEN`)
- **Request:** `GET /workspaces` with
  `Authorization: Bearer 1ad7c91e...10e3d4` (the old vestigial `PRIVATE_AUTH_TOKEN`
  value, never issued by `/users/password-authenticate`).
- **Response:** status `401`, body empty string.
- **Why:** `AuthToken.findOne({token, status:'active'})` → `null` →
  `getUserByAccessToken` throws `'No user found by the userId from the
  access token'`. The thrown error is swallowed by `authReq` and converted
  to an empty-body 401.

> **Important corollary — PRIVATE_AUTH_TOKEN is dead code.**  
> `src/server.js:155` has `const privateAuthToken = ENV.PRIVATE_AUTH_TOKEN`.
> Grep across `src/**/*.js` finds exactly **two** references to that
> constant: the declaration and the env loader (`envServer.js`). **No
> middleware, no route, no handler ever compares against it.**
> The stale value in Infisical (`LIVEDEMO_API_TOKEN == PRIVATE_AUTH_TOKEN
> == 1ad7c91e...`) existed only because a prior operator copied the string
> into both secrets. The only auth mechanism is user-issued tokens.

### Authorized request
- **Request:** `GET /workspaces` with
  `Authorization: Bearer 2a163442...e1d0d2`.
- **Response:** status `200`, body is a JSON array of workspace documents
  the authenticated user belongs to. See `discovery-log.md` §Phase-0 for
  the literal body.

### Workspace access check (secondary authorization layer)
Most resource routes call
`helpers.validateUserHasAccessToWorkspace(authUserDoc, workspaceId)` after
`authReq`. This checks that `workspaceId` is in `authUserDoc.workspaces`.
A valid token for a user who is **not** a member of the workspace in the
URL → the thrown "Access denied" error surfaces as a 500 with body
`'{"error":"..."}'` (not a 403 — the backend's error normalization is
imperfect). This is the one place the caller must be careful: using the
bot user's token against a workspace it doesn't belong to will return
500, not 401/403.

## UNVERIFIED blocks

> ⚠ UNVERIFIED: The `authtokens` collection's index list has not been
> captured from live Mongo in this session (the backend container went
> serverless-idle before the planned mongosh pass). Source shows no
> TTL/`expireAfterSeconds` index defined in Mongoose; if ops manually
> created one, it would not be visible here. To verify:
> ```
> railway ssh --service livedemo-backend
> node -e 'const {MongoClient}=require("mongodb"); (async()=>{const c=new MongoClient(process.env.MONGO_URI); await c.connect(); console.log(await c.db().collection("authtokens").indexes()); await c.close()})()'
> ```

> ⚠ UNVERIFIED: `POST /users/refreshToken` was not exercised in this
> session. Its request body and response shape are assumed from
> `postRefreshToken.js` but not confirmed with a live call. Document
> before the MCP rewrite adds any refresh loop that depends on it.
